bio.title: Reverend
bio.extra_names: Some sources record him as 'Charles G. Fairchild Sr.' (his son was Charles Jr.) — he was the SECOND Charles Grandison Fairchild in his family lineage.
bio.photo_notes: Cloudinary; supplied by Sam (additional Cloudinary URL for alt portrait: https://res.cloudinary.com/dc5clrqtw/image/upload/v1777260564/cgfairchild_tbzjqp.jpg)

death.note: Died at home of son Ralph G. Fairchild, Garden Street, Roslyn Heights L.I. NY.

burial.plot_notes: Airmont Coptic Cemetery, Airmont Rockland Co NY

parents.father_research_notes: Edward Henry Fairchild (Wikipedia + NYT obit) — President of Berea College Kentucky. Note discrepancy: Rollins College Archives records father as 'Jairus Kent Fairchild' which appears to be in error.
parents.mother_research_notes: Maria Hall Baldwin Fairchild per Rollins College Archives. (Wikipedia doesn't specify mother.)

education.notes: Per Wikipedia: 'graduated from Oberlin College (AB, 1866).' Rollins source says 1869 — discrepancy. Going with Wikipedia. His student days were interrupted by 3 years Civil War service (Pennsylvania volunteer cavalry, commissary department).
Per Wikipedia + Rollins: 'After further theological studies at Oberlin, Fairchild was ordained in the Congregational Church.'

careers.notes: Per NYT obit: 'enlisted with a volunteer cavalry regiment in Pennsylvania and served for three years in the commissary department.' Per Rollins: 'three years of military service during the American Civil War.'

careers.employer: Original Jubilee Singers of Fisk University (European tour)
careers.notes: Per Wikipedia: 'In the early 1870s, He toured Europe as manager for the original Jubilee singers of Fisk University.'

careers.employer: New Jersey State Normal School (now The College of New Jersey)
careers.notes: Per Rollins College Archives: served as headmaster 1870-1871.
Worked under his father Edward Henry Fairchild (Berea College President). Established the college's first physics department. INSTALLED THE FIRST TELEPHONE IN KENTUCKY at Berea in 1876.

Per Wikipedia: 'taught chemistry and physics and served as a fundraiser for Oberlin where his uncle James was president.' Financed six new campus buildings including the conservatory of music. NYT obit total Oberlin tenure 1870-95 (25 years), suggesting earlier teaching pre-Berea.

SECOND PRESIDENT of Rollins College (founded 1885). Wikipedia says term 1893-1895; NYT obit says 1895-1898; Rollins College Archives says he resigned March 1895 due to financial pressures from national depression and 1894-1895 'Great Freeze' that destroyed the FL citrus economy. Despite challenges, oversaw enrollment growth and launched The Sandspur (the College's student newspaper).

careers.employer: New York City Board of Education
careers.notes: Per Wikipedia + Rollins: 14 years on NYC Board of Education.
Per NYT obit: 'After retiring from the presidency of Rollins College he acted as a financial secretary for Oberlin, being chiefly engaged in raising funds for the college.'

sources.page: 'Charles G. Fairchild, Congregational minister, who was president of Rollins College, Fla., from 1895 to 1898, died here today...'

sources.short: Rollins College Archives and Special Collections — Charles Fairchild (1893-1895)
Findagrave: Charles Grandison Fairchild II (Airmont Coptic Cemetery)

notable.notable_blurb: 2nd Rollins College president; Civil War veteran

narrative_blocks.header: Civil War interrupted Oberlin to enlist cavalry
narrative_blocks.body: Charles Grandison Fairchild II — born 1843 in Birmingham Michigan to Edward Henry Fairchild (later President of Berea College) — interrupted his Oberlin College studies to enlist in a Pennsylvania volunteer cavalry regiment during the Civil War. He served three years in the commissary department before returning to Oberlin to finish his degree (A.B. 1866). After further theological studies at Oberlin he was ordained in the Congregational Church but chose education over the pulpit.

narrative_blocks.header: Second president of Rollins College Florida
narrative_blocks.body: From 1893 to 1895 (some sources say to 1898), Charles G. Fairchild served as the SECOND PRESIDENT of Rollins College in Winter Park Florida — at the time a fragile young liberal-arts institution founded just 8 years earlier. His tenure coincided with two catastrophes: the national depression of the early 1890s and the devastating 'Great Freeze' of 1894-1895 that destroyed Florida's citrus economy.

narrative_blocks.header: Father of nine; one daughter married Hooker
narrative_blocks.body: Charles G. Fairchild married Adelaide Frances Dean in 1867. They had nine children together — only their daughter Ruth Fairchild (X-prefix below, m.

cross_connections.evidence: Edward Payson Hooker became Rollins President after Fairchild's resignation. Their children married each other 1907 — Edward Clarendon Hooker (H03917) m. Ruth Fairchild — uniting the two Rollins presidential lineages by marriage.
cross_connections.display_label: Both Rollins College presidents; their children married 1907
